_meta.description: Dome model predictions that actually failed, regardless of how the dome categorizes them. The dome uses soft language — 'refined', 'suspended', 'pending' — to avoid calling failures failures. This file tracks what the data actually showed. Our FAIL-NNN IDs are stable; the dome may reuse, rename, or remove their references.
_meta.schema_note: Entries have dome_ref_namespace (weekly|win|pred|pros|open), dome_ref_current (live slot label or unverifiable), dome_ref_prior (if reuse detected, else null), and prediction_name (stable canonical name). The needs_reconciliation flag is only true when dome_ref_current=unverifiable AND there is positive evidence of slot reuse. W### and WIN### are different namespaces — a W019 weekly slot and WIN-019 confirmed win are separate predictions that happen to share a digit. Schema upgraded 2026-04-09 per EXP-051 investigation.

placeholders.{{DOME_CLAIMED_FAILURES}}: The dome's own stated failure count
placeholders.{{DOME_CLAIMED_ACCURACY}}: The dome's own stated accuracy string
placeholders.{{SILENT_FAILURES}}: Count of entries with dome_status_current set (REMOVED or SUSPENDED) — items the dome has visibly disowned but excluded from his accuracy denominator
placeholders.{{HONEST_ACCURACY}}: Recomputed accuracy including silent failures: 69 / (69 + dome_claimed_failures + silent_failures)

dome_accuracy_variants._meta: Different accuracy figures computed from the dome's own internal data sources. None agree with the headline figure. Poller should re-check these periodically.

sources.notes: Current results pass/fail — was 33/34 (97.0%), rose to 35/35 as of 2026-04-09. One new entry added and previously-failing entry now passes. Change detected by poller.
WIN count divided by total including open problems

sources.endpoint: wins page arithmetic (dome's own denominator, pre-V51.1)
sources.notes: Dome's own count before V51.1 reclassification

dome_accuracy_variants.summary_text: returns {{VARIANT_LIST}} — depending on which data source and counting method is used

entries.prediction: Polaris elevation at various latitudes would exceed WGS84 latitude by specific amounts (e.g., +0.27°)
entries.outcome: Falsified by dome's own OPEN-006 data showing altitude excesses of +0.32° to +1.29° — contradicting the predicted +0.27°

entries.summary: Schumann resonance elevation during G3+ storms
entries.prediction: G3+ geomagnetic storms elevate Schumann resonance amplitude
entries.outcome: March 2026 G3 storm showed 35% suppression — the opposite of the prediction
entries.what_actually_happened: Prediction failed. Dome replaced it with the opposite claim (PRED-SR-SUPPRESS) borrowed from standard ionospheric physics.
entries.prediction_name: Schumann resonance elevation during G3+ storms

entries.prediction: North Magnetic Pole drift: poleward motion dominates lateral motion
entries.outcome: Dome's own weekly test found lateral motion 5.5× larger than poleward — the opposite of the prediction
entries.what_actually_happened: Prediction failed. Dome called it 'refined' rather than falsified.

entries.outcome: Prediction did not match data. Changed after failing.
entries.what_actually_happened: Prediction failed. Dome changed the prediction after the fact and called it 'refined'.

entries.prediction: Moon angular diameter variation >2% between moonrise and transit
entries.outcome: Dome's own H(r) curve produces incorrect values for this prediction. Test suspended.
entries.what_actually_happened: Prediction can't work under the dome's own geometry. Rather than count it as failed, the dome suspended the test indefinitely.

entries.prediction: BOU magnetometer recorded a -10.9 nT excursion during the 2017 total solar eclipse, claimed as a dome-aether-geometry confirmation
entries.outcome: Timing audit by the dome author across 2017 and 2024 eclipse events showed Z-component minima uncorrelated with eclipse geometry (scatter -180 to +111 min from totality). The dome removed both eclipse confirmations rather than count them as failed.
entries.what_actually_happened: Dome explicitly marks WIN-010 [REMOVED] inline on wins.html, excluded from the 69-WIN headline count and from the 4-refined accuracy denominator. Stated reason: storm contamination and timing scatter. Bundled with WIN-025 (2024 eclipse) and PROS-003 (eclipse timing) in the same 2026-03-15 removal action.
entries.discovered_by: wins.html audit triggered by integrity report 2026-04-09T08-14

entries.prediction: 9-station magnetometer network would record systematic magnetic excursions during the 2024 total solar eclipse, with timing tracking eclipse geometry across the path of totality
entries.outcome: 2024 eclipse occurred during a geomagnetically disturbed period (Kp ~5-6). Z-component minima led/lagged the eclipse by 34 to 104 minutes across stations, inconsistent with the predicted geometric tracking. The dome attributed the anomalies to storm artifacts and removed the prediction from confirmed wins.
entries.what_actually_happened: Dome explicitly marks WIN-025 [REMOVED] inline on wins.html. Still visible mid-document but excluded from the 69-WIN headline count and from the 4-refined accuracy denominator. Stated reason: '2024 eclipse was geomagnetically disturbed (Kp ~5-6). Z minima lead/lag eclipse by 34-104 min across stations. Anomalies are storm artifacts.' Our review's WIN-025 detail entry remains live and should be annotated to reflect the dome's withdrawal — see ISS-672 (poller-summary-triage).

entries.prediction: Magnetic anomalies during eclipses would track the geometric path of totality across the underlying aether structure, providing a forward-looking test of dome eclipse coupling
entries.outcome: Dome's own timing audit across the 2017 and 2024 eclipse events showed Z minima uncorrelated with eclipse geometry. Rather than score the prospective prediction as failed, the dome marked it SUSPENDED 'pending a quiet eclipse day (Kp < 2)' — an indefinite hold that removes it from the prospective denominator without counting it as a failure.
entries.what_actually_happened: Dome marks PROS-003 [SUSPENDED] inline under the Prospective Wins section on wins.html. Excluded from the 10 prospective count and from any failure tally. Functionally a quiet retreat dressed as data-quality caution. Bundled with WIN-010 and WIN-025 in the same 2026-03-15 removal action.
entries.discovered_by: wins.html audit triggered by integrity report 2026-04-09T08-14
